text.title: Prompt Basics — Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 known failure modes when authoring prompts in production. Each carries symptom / root-cause / fix.

failure-mode.description: Silent regression — prompt unchanged in git but model version upgraded; outputs change shape; downstream parsers break days later.
failure-mode.detector: Snapshot tests of `template.render(**fixture).content` diverge from baseline after model upgrade.
failure-mode.repair: Snapshot every PromptTemplate against the current model in CI. Block deploys on snapshot diff; review and re-pin.

failure-mode.description: Prompt injection — user-supplied text contains "ignore previous instructions" and the model complies, breaking constraints.
failure-mode.detector: Output drifts from system constraints when fuzzing user_template with adversarial inputs.
failure-mode.repair: Render via `user_template.format(...)`; never concatenate user text into system role. Add an input filter that strips role-marker tokens (`system:`, `assistant:`).

failure-mode.description: Runtime concat — `prompt = f"Do X with {user_value}"` constructed at call time; not committed; impossible to diff.
failure-mode.detector: Code review finds f-strings or `.format` calls outside the PromptTemplate boundary.
failure-mode.repair: Refactor to a PromptTemplate constant. Lint rule that flags `chat.completions.create(messages=[{"content": f"..."} ...])` in non-template files.

failure-mode.description: Vague task description — "summarise this" — yields output length and tone that vary call-to-call.
failure-mode.detector: Output length variance / format variance > 30% across 20 calls on identical input.
failure-mode.repair: Replace with explicit instruction: "Summarise in exactly 3 bullet points, each ≤ 15 words, factual tone."

failure-mode.description: Missing system role — `messages=[{"role":"user", "content": "..."}]` only — model behaviour drifts across versions.
failure-mode.repair: PromptTemplate constructor rejects empty system. Validator script flags missing system in rendered output.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: The user_template's `{var}` placeholders are the ONLY surface where user content enters. Any other interpolation route is a vulnerability. Validators must inspect every commit for f-strings inside message construction.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Any prompt change that affects a production output is reviewed by a named engineer, with snapshot diffs attached to the PR. Prompt drift causes silent breakages — review is the only guardrail before deploy.
